appraisal_events.evidence: « Mais après il l'avait eue à lui, devenue sa femme, et jusqu'à ce qu'il mourût... Mais enfin Albertine, je ne l'avais pas gardée comme il avait gardé Odette. »
appraisal_events.explanation: The narrator contrasts his own failure to keep Albertine with Swann’s eventual lasting possession of Odette through marriage, locally placing Swann as more successful in securing the beloved.

status_effects.explanation: Within this comparison, Swann is positioned as having achieved enduring intimacy (marriage) that the narrator failed to maintain.
